acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] WalIndex::record_event does not accept an unused request_sha256 parameter.
- [ ] Call sites and tests updated; no unused variables remain.
description:
**Problem**
WalIndexTxn::record_event accepts request_sha256 but immediately discards it (let _ = request_sha256). Callers still pass a value, implying it is stored. This hides information loss and is confusing (error_design principle: preserve info until an explicit boundary).

**Files:** src/daemon/wal/index.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs, src/daemon/core.rs
design:
**Design**
- Remove request_sha256 from the WalIndex trait method signature and all call sites, OR store it in the events table if it is truly needed.
- Prefer removal (spec says request_sha256 belongs to client_requests; event index is for WAL offsets).
- Update tests and any documentation/comments accordingly.
title: Remove unused request_sha256 from WalIndex::record_event

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Dry-run refuses unrelated local/remote store histories without `--force`.
- [ ] Dry-run with `--force` still previews the rewrite non-mutatingly.
- [ ] Tests cover the dry-run divergence refusal path.
closed_reason: Dry-run now refuses unrelated local/remote histories without --force and tests cover the non-mutating forced preview path.
description:
**Problem**
The latest adversarial review found that `bd migrate to 1 --dry-run` no longer models the execute path's unrelated-history refusal gate. The new preview path skips the common-ancestor check entirely, so dry-run can report a normal success-shaped preview for a repo that real execution will reject with `NoCommonAncestor` unless rerun with `--force`.

That makes the documented dry-run-first rollout flow unsafe.

**Files**: crates/beads-git/src/sync.rs, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs, MIGRATION.md
design:
**Design**
Apply the same unrelated-divergence safety policy in preview that execute enforces after source resolution. Dry-run should still stay non-mutating, but if local and remote histories are unrelated and `--force` was not requested, preview must return the same refusal instead of a clean success payload. Keep `--force` behavior consistent between preview and execute.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ 1ac791c3: dry-run now enforces the same unrelated-history safety gate as execute. `preview_migrate_store_ref_to_v1()` checks local/remote ancestry before returning a success-shaped preview, refuses with `NoCommonAncestor` without `--force`, and keeps `--force --dry-run` non-mutating. Coverage now includes the unrelated-divergence dry-run refusal and forced preview path in the migration slow suite.

Verification:
- cargo fmt --all
- just dylint
- cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings
- cargo test
- cargo test -p beads-rs migration --features slow-tests -- --nocapture
- cargo test --features slow-tests (still fails only on the pre-existing SUN_LEN REPL harness bug tracked by beads-rs-iymc)

title: Migration follow-up: make dry-run enforce the same unrelated-divergence safety gate as execute

description:
**Problem**
Go beads supports tracks dependencies for convoy issues. Convoys track completion of multiple items across projects with reactive completion semantics. This is non-blocking cross-project reference tracking. beads-rs needs this.

**How it works in Go beads**
- Dependency type: `DepTracks` (in internal/types/types.go)
- Convoy issue type tracks multiple issues
- Non-blocking: doesn't prevent work from starting
- Reactive: convoy can auto-close when all tracked items close
- Used for cross-project coordination without blocking

**Design for Rust**
1. Add `Tracks` variant to `DepKind` enum in src/core/domain.rs
2. Mark it as non-DAG-enforcing (non-blocking)
3. Add convoy issue type (separate bead, see parent epic)
4. Consider: convoy auto-closure logic
   - When all tracked items close, close convoy?
   - Or require manual closure?

**Design considerations**
- Directionality: convoy -> tracked-issue (Go's choice)
- Query needs: "list all items tracked by convoy X"
- Should tracks support tracking external refs?
  * Enables cross-repo convoy tracking
- Convoy completion predicate: all closed? any closed? custom?

**Design questions for human review**
- Should tracks trigger notifications when items close?
- Should we support weighted tracking (some items more important)?
- Should convoy type be required for tracks deps?
  * Or allow any bead to track others?
- Auto-closure timing: immediate on last item? batched?

**Files to study**
- tmp/beads/internal/types/types.go - DepTracks, TypeConvoy
- tmp/beads/cmd/bd/dep.go - tracks usage in dep list
- Go beads convoy implementation (if exists)

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Tracks added to DepKind enum
- [ ] Non-blocking (doesn't affect ready work)
- [ ] Can query "what does convoy X track"
- [ ] Supports convoy -> issue directionality
- [ ] Cargo fmt, clippy, test pass

**Note**
This is foundational for convoy support. Full convoy features (auto-closure, reactive completion) likely need separate beads.
title: Add tracks dependency type for convoy cross-project tracking

description:
**Problem**
`crates/beads-daemon/src/testkit/e2e.rs` changed `NetworkProfile::tailnet()` from a degraded/lossy profile into a latency-only profile during `beads-rs-fblt.15`. Existing callers such as `crates/beads-rs/tests/e2e.rs` still read like tailnet-fault coverage, so the API change is hidden and coverage outside the new `LinkFaultProfile` path is weaker than before.

**Design**
Make the deterministic harness tailnet/degraded profile semantics explicit again: either restore the old degraded semantics where the API name promises them, or rename/split the helper surface and update callers/tests so the coverage intent is obvious and preserved.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Tailnet/degraded deterministic network helpers have explicit, truthful semantics
- [ ] `crates/beads-rs/tests/e2e.rs` still proves the intended fault/latency surface instead of silently weakening it
- [ ] Narrow tests cover the chosen helper semantics

**Files**
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/testkit/e2e.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/e2e.rs`
title: Resolve NetworkProfile::tailnet semantic drift after deterministic harness migration

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] DAG docs and lint policy match the final crate graph
- [ ] Non-CLI daemon coupling inventory is refreshed after the cutover
- [ ] Full verification matrix from the plan passes
- [ ] Any tracker follow-up needed for stale/open historical epics is captured explicitly
description:
**Problem**
The previous crate split left some tracker/docs state stale: older epics still read as open even when they report 100% done, and the current boundary docs/inventory need a post-cutover refresh. We also need one explicit closeout bead that owns the full verification matrix and final inventory update.

**Where**
- `docs/CRATE_DAG.md`
- `docs/architecture/non-cli-daemon-coupling-inventory.md`
- `lints/beads_lints/tests/crate_dag_policy.rs`
- full workspace verification commands from the approved plan

**Why It Matters**
Without a dedicated closeout bead, the implementation may stop after code compiles and miss the durable verification/docs work that makes the cleanup understandable to future agents.

**Files to Study**
- `docs/CRATE_DAG.md`
- `docs/architecture/non-cli-daemon-coupling-inventory.md`
- `lints/beads_lints/tests/crate_dag_policy.rs`
- `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-13-final-crate-boundary-cleanup.md`
design: After the code movement is done, update the DAG docs, refresh the non-CLI daemon coupling inventory, and run the full verification matrix from the approved plan (`cargo fmt`, `just dylint`, `cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo test --features slow-tests`, `cargo check -p beads-rs --all-features`). If older tracker items need administrative cleanup, capture that explicitly rather than hand-waving it.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Final closeout audit on 2026-03-13 from /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs.

Docs/inventory refresh landed in jj commit `ab7a92ae` (`beads-rs-gou3.8: refresh docs and run closeout verification`). Verification-driven follow-ons landed in `4dbf5b8c` (`beads-rs-gou3.8.1`), `dc435d3d` (`beads-rs-fgwn`), and `42f4dc50` (`beads-rs-gou3.8.2`).

What closed here:
- refreshed `docs/CRATE_DAG.md` to describe `beads-rs` as a tiny assembly crate and documented CLI/test ownership invariants
- refreshed `docs/architecture/non-cli-daemon-coupling-inventory.md` to the post-cutover state with no remaining `beads_daemon::runtime::*` or `beads_daemon::git::*` imports
- ran the full closeout matrix successfully on the current stack
- captured the only non-blocking residual from adversarial review as `beads-rs-vi7x`
- administratively closed stale-open historical epics `bd-14fs` and `bd-6ea1` after re-audit

Verification:
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `just dylint`
- `cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`
- `cargo test --features slow-tests`
- `cargo check -p beads-rs --all-features`

title: Refresh boundary docs/inventory and run the final verification closeout
